title: {'error': 'Live search is deprecated. Please switch to the Agent Tools API: https://docs.x.ai/docs/guides/tools/overview'}

title: [Security] Memory content injected into system prompt without sanitization enables indirect prompt injection
evidence: lite_agent.py:568-581 — recalled memory content concatenated unsanitized into system message; poisoned tool outputs persist as memories and elevate to system-prompt authority on next session
notes: textbook indirect prompt injection across session boundaries. Two failure modes overlap here: AF-009 (untrusted content reinterpreted as instructions) and AF-006 (poisoned memory corrupts future agent context). Best find in the queue — a real CVE-shaped writeup against a major framework.

title: RFC: Detecting silent behavioral drift in agents across session boundaries
evidence: RFC body: 'agents... silently change behavior after context compression or memory rotation — without triggering exceptions or failed tasks. The agent completes the work, but the behavioral fingerprint has shifted.'
notes: not an observed incident — it's an RFC describing the AF-006 mechanism with three measurable signals (lexicon decay, tool-call sequence shift, semantic drift) and a reference impl (compression-monitor). Useful design-partner evidence: the community is *asking* for what Mycelium proposes to build. Low confidence because hypothetical, not a reproducible bug.

title: Fail closed on checkpoint schema validation before load
evidence: issue body: 'Invalid checkpoint payloads can reach load paths without a strict fail-closed schema gate, creating undefined runtime behavior and brittle recovery semantics... validation strictness is not consistently fail-closed at load boundary.'
notes: hardening request — without this, a malformed checkpoint can corrupt agent state on resume. Same shape as the langchain `except: pass` finding: structural mechanism evidence for AF-006, not an observed incident.

title: preemptive_generation=True causes duplicate LLM requests and doubled token costs
notes: duplicate LLM calls / wasted tokens. Not AF-003 — that requires an actual loop, this is a single duplicate. Library config / cancellation bug.

title: core: inconsistent behavior for `BaseTool` subclasses that return `list` from `_run`/`_arun`
notes: library API ambiguity: empty list from _run treated as 'already a list of content blocks' rather than serialized. Strong writeup but it's a framework API design bug, not observed agent failure. Could be flipped to AF-004 (Tool Misuse) if you want to stretch — would be low-confidence at best.

title: Better agent logging
evidence: issue body: 'agent-level calls bypass the logging infrastructure entirely. The logInferenceToFile feature only captures calls made through Stagehand's inference.ts functions... [but] AnthropicCUAClient: Uses client.beta.messages.create() directly... bypass the logging infrastructure entirely.'
notes: second AF-002 mechanism finding in this corpus. Same shape as the langchain except-pass: a major framework's agent layer makes LLM calls invisible to the observability system. Useful complement to the langchain finding for design-partner pitch (multi-vendor pattern).

title: Runtime evidence bundles for multi-agent execution
notes: vendor pitch (agent-evidence runtime bundles). Worth noting it pitches an AF-002 solution, so confirms market demand exists, but it's not an incident.

title: Cline doesn’t recognize that Act mode is active.
evidence: issue body: 'Cline repeatedly asks to switch from Plan mode to Act mode, even though Act mode is already active. Steps to reproduce: 1. Work on a single task for several iterations until the prompt size... reaches at least 100k-120k tokens.'
notes: real user-facing failure with reproducible trigger (long context). Agent's working state diverges from real state — textbook AF-006. Strongest behavioral incident in the queue.

title: When invoking the tool-call subgraph, the main agent loses the memory of previous tool invocations.
evidence: issue body: 'When invoking the tool-call subgraph, the main agent loses the memory of previous tool invocations.'
notes: title and description match AF-006, but the repro code uses non-existent imports (`from langgraph import LangGraph`, `langgraph.actors`) — looks AI-generated and not actually runnable. Mechanism plausible but evidence weak. Low confidence.
